title: Multiply using groups of objects
topic_path: Get ready for multiplying and dividing fractions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following image to find the total number of eggs. **


[[☃ image 1]]

$9$ groups of $5 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$9 \times 5 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
So, let's skip count by $\maroonD{\text{five}}$ $\blueD9$ times to find the total.


$\overset{\blueD1}{\maroonD{5}}{} , \overset{\blueD2}{\maroonD{10}}{} , \overset{\blueD3}{\maroonD{15}}{} , \overset{\blueD4}{\maroonD{20}}{}, \overset{\blueD5}{\maroonD{25}}{}, \overset{\blueD6}{\maroonD{30}}{}, \overset{\blueD7}{\maroonD{35}}{} , \overset{\blueD8}{\maroonD{40}}{}, \overset{\blueD9}{\greenD{45}}{}$


$\blueD9\text{ groups of }\maroonD{5} = \greenD{45}$

We can also express $\blueD9\text{ groups of }\maroonD{5}$ using multiplication.  

$\blueD9\text{ groups of }\maroonD{5} =\blueD9\times\maroonD5$

options.alt: Nine groups of 5 eggs. Each group has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following image to find the total number of hot dogs. **

[[☃ image 1]]

$8$ groups of $2 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$8 \times 2 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueD8$ groups of $\maroonD{{2}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]

[[☃ image 7]]

[[☃ image 8]]

So, let's skip count by $\maroonD{\text{two}}$ $\blueD8$ times to find the total.


$\overset{\blueD1}{\maroonD{2}}{} , \overset{\blueD2}{\maroonD{4}}{} , \overset{\blueD3}{\maroonD{6}}{} , \overset{\blueD4}{\maroonD{8}}{}, \overset{\blueD5}{\maroonD{10}}{} , \overset{\blueD6}{\maroonD{12}}{} , \overset{\blueD7}{\maroonD{14}}{} , \overset{\blueD8}{\greenD{16}}$


$\blueD8\text{ groups of }\maroonD{{2}} = \greenD{16}$

We can also express $\blueD8\text{ groups of }\maroonD{{2}}$ using multiplication.  

$\blueD8\text{ groups of }\maroonD{{2}} =\blueD8\times\maroonD2$

options.alt: Eight groups of 2 hot dogs. Each group has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following image to help find the total number of rabbits. **

[[☃ image 1]]

$9\text{ fours} ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$9 \times 4 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
There are $\blueD9$ groups of $\maroonD4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]

[[☃ image 7]]

[[☃ image 8]]

[[☃ image 9]]

So, let's skip count by $\maroonD{\text{four}}$ $\blueD9$ times to find the total.

$\overset{\blueD1}{\maroonD{4}}{} , \overset{\blueD2}{\maroonD{8}}{} , \overset{\blueD3}{\maroonD{12}}{} , \overset{\blueD4}{\maroonD{16}}{}, \overset{\blueD5}{\maroonD{20}}{} , \overset{\blueD6}{\maroonD{24}}{} , \overset{\blueD7}{\maroonD{28}}{} , \overset{\blueD8}{\maroonD{32}}{},\overset{\blueD9}{\greenD{36}}$

$\blueD9\maroonD{\text{ fours}} = \greenD{36}$

We can also express $\blueD9$ groups of $\maroonD{4}$ using multiplication.  

$\blueD9\text{ groups of }\maroonD{{4}} =\blueD9\times\maroonD4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following image to help find the total number of glasses of milk. **


[[☃ image 1]]

$10\text{ twos} ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$10 \times 2 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
So, let's skip count by $\maroonD{\text{two}}$ $\blueD{10}$ times to find the total.

$\overset{\blueD1}{\maroonD{2}}{}, \overset{\blueD2}{\maroonD{4}} , \overset{\blueD3}{\maroonD{6}}{}, \overset{\blueD4}{\maroonD{8}}{} , \overset{\blueD5}{\maroonD{10}}{} , \overset{\blueD6}{\maroonD{12}} , \overset{\blueD7}{\maroonD{14}}{} , \overset{\blueD8}{\maroonD{16}}, \overset{\blueD9}{\maroonD{18}}, \overset{\blueD{10}}{\greenD{20}}{}$

$\blueD{10}\maroonD{\text{ twos}} = \greenD{20}$

We can also express $\blueD{10}$ groups of $\maroonD{2}$ using multiplication.  

$\blueD{10}\text{ groups of }\maroonD{{2}} =\blueD{10}\times\maroonD{2}$

options.alt: Ten groups of 2 glasses of milk. Each group of glasses has a circle around it.
